hard.next_step: Align with Gladys on what Seminole County wants to improve across purchasing, supplier access, and spend visibility. Review the current request to PO process and identify where approvals, controls, and user adoption need improvement. Understand how suppliers currently register, update information, share documents, and communicate with the county. Define the exact reporting views needed across spend, suppliers, categories, compliance, and department level activity. Run a tailored walkthrough showing how eProcurement, iSupplier,
hard.ais_why:
* Seminole County needs a structured procurement platform to "improve request handling, supplier access, and spend visibility" - a foundational public-sector procurement requirement.
* The driver is structuring the request-to-PO process and reporting, not AI capability.
* Next step is to "review current request to PO process and define reporting views"; Merlin and ML iAnalyze are in scope but AI is not part of the stated requirement. Qualified at 10%.

north_star_verdict.headline: Created 28 May, this inbound P2P deal never started: no buyer conversation has taken place, the only named contact (Gladys Marrozos) has not answered any outreach, and the 13 Oct close is a placeholder with no discovery, pain, budget owner, or procurement path established.
north_star_verdict.math: 99 days to close (13 Oct 2026); created 28 May 2026, 39 days ago. Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 28 May and has not moved since. Zero time-in-stage progression: no submission dates, no field-history movement beyond creation, and LastActivityDate is null across the full 39-day lifecycle. Five rep call attempts logged 29 May went unanswered. A US county-government P2P purchase must still run discovery, likely a formal RFP, vendor shortlisting, evaluation, InfoSec, funding approval, and contracting, none of which has begun, so 99 days is not credible.

metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified impact has been articulated by the buyer, because no discovery conversation has occurred since the deal was created 28 May. The only signal is the rep-authored Next_Step__c and AIS_Why__c, which infer a foundational public-sector need to 'improve request handling, supplier access, and spend visibility' and to structure the request-to-PO process, but no cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings target has been stated by anyone at Seminole County. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, and Value_to_Customer__c are all empty. The move is to secure a live discovery call with Gladys Marrozos and quantify the request-to-PO and spend-visibility pain in the county's own numbers.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. Gladys Marrozos is tagged Decision Maker but her title is Lead Procurement Administrator (Manager), a procurement-operations manager rather than the budget authority for a $100k platform investment in county government. No call, task, or field (Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all empty) shows who controls the funding, and there is no conversation to infer it from. A US county P2P purchase of this size typically needs Finance and IT sign-off and possibly Board or County-Administrator approval on a fiscal cycle. Identifying and reaching the budget owner is a ranked move.
sources: Task 29 May: 'Decision Maker is not identified'

decision_criteria.narrative: The county's evaluation criteria are unknown because no discovery, demo, RFI, or workshop has taken place. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are unpopulated, and there is no call content to read criteria from. The only inferred priorities are those in the rep's Next_Step__c agenda (approvals, controls, user adoption, supplier registration, and reporting views across spend, suppliers, categories, compliance, and department activity), which are hypotheses, not confirmed buyer criteria. Confirming these against the county's stated requirements is the first discovery objective.

decision_process.narrative: There is no observable decision process because there has been no call sequence to read it from: no meetings, no stakeholders joining or escalating, no approval chain surfaced. What_is_the_decision_process__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are empty. For a Florida county government the likely path is a formal solicitation (RFP or piggyback contract) with cross-departmental review and possibly Board approval, but none of this has been confirmed with the buyer. Mapping the county's procurement path and approval chain is a required early move.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown. No MSA, order form, SOW, legal, or InfoSec discussion exists on any call or field, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so there is no incumbent-renewal forcing function. County technology purchases usually require formal procurement (solicitation or cooperative contract, vendor registration, insurance certificates, W-9, and possibly Board approval), none of which is scoped. Visibility here starts only once a first buyer conversation establishes whether the county will buy via RFP, an existing cooperative vehicle, or sole source.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been voiced by Seminole County. The inferred pain, structuring the request-to-PO process and improving supplier access and spend visibility, comes only from the rep's Next_Step__c and AIS_Why__c, not from a buyer statement, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c are all empty. The pain owner is also unmapped: whether Gladys owns the pain or is a messenger for Finance, IT, or county leadership is unknown. Validating the pain and its owner directly with the county is prerequisite to any business case.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Gladys Marrozos is the sole contact and is tagged Decision Maker, but there is zero evidence of engagement, let alone advocacy: no call, no email exchange, no task completion, and five 'Call Attempted' logs on 29 May that went unanswered. There is no sign she has introduced other stakeholders, shared internal context, or is aware Zycus is pursuing this. The deal is single-threaded into a non-responsive contact; the first move is for Grace Kim to convert Gladys from a name on the record into an engaged coach through a live discovery conversation.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Gladys Marrozos, Decision Maker (no engagement date)

competition.narrative: The competitive picture is unknown because discovery never happened, not because the deal is confirmed sole-source. Competitors__c and the free-text competition fields (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c) are all empty, and there is no call content naming a rival or an incumbent. For a US county-government P2P purchase this is likely to become a formal multi-vendor evaluation (or a piggyback off an existing cooperative contract), so what the county runs today and who else they may consider is a first-call discovery objective, not a settled advantage.

deal_movement.summary: No genuine buyer movement since creation. The record shows only the 28 May Initial-Interest-to-Qualified stage flip by Grace Kim, a 01 Jun no-op Next_Step edit, and a Type-to-New-Business change on 29 May; every 29 May task is a rep-side 'Call Attempted' health flag, and no buyer has responded on any channel through 06 Jul.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, single-threaded early deal that never got a first conversation; the win-strategy priorities are to restart engagement, break single-thread into Finance and IT, and reframe the value away from AI.
flags: Re-engage the buyer first: Gladys Marrozos has ignored five 29 May call attempts, so a fresh owner-led outreach with a value-specific reason to meet is the only thing that creates signal.
Break single-thread before demoing: only Gladys is mapped and unengaged, so identify and reach the Finance budget owner and an IT stakeholder to find real power for a $100k county purchase.
Lead with request-to-PO structuring value, not Merlin: AIS is Negative because the county's driver is process and reporting, so an AI-forward pitch misfires.

Until the buyer expresses AI appetite, the Merlin/I2O story is over-positioned relative to the actual need.
